# Implementation Plan: Add GitHub Copilot Slash Commands to Solune Chat

**Branch**: `001-copilot-slash-commands` | **Date**: 2026-03-25 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-copilot-slash-commands/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Add 14 new slash commands to Solune Chat mirroring applicable GitHub Copilot CLI slash commands. Commands are split into two categories: **Local** commands (`/clear`, `/feedback`, `/share`, `/experimental`) that execute on the frontend with optional backend calls, and **Passthrough** commands (`/model`, `/compact`, `/context`, `/diff`, `/usage`, `/mcp`, `/plan`) that forward to the backend AI service. The existing command registry pattern (registry.ts + handler modules) is extended with new handler files organized by domain. HelpPage and `/help` auto-update since they already read from the command registry.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript ~5.9 (frontend), Python >=3.12 (backend)
**Primary Dependencies**: React 19, Vite 8, TanStack Query 5, FastAPI, Pydantic 2, github-copilot-sdk
**Storage**: SQLite (aiosqlite) for backend persistence, browser localStorage for client-side settings
**Testing**: Vitest (frontend unit), Playwright (E2E), pytest + pytest-asyncio (backend)
**Target Platform**: Web application (browser frontend, Linux server backend)
**Project Type**: Web (frontend + backend monorepo under `solune/`)
**Performance Goals**: Local commands <100ms, passthrough commands <5s response (SC-006), /clear <2s for 500 messages (SC-003), /share <3s for 200 messages (SC-005)
**Constraints**: Rate limit 10 messages/min on backend send_message endpoint, 75% backend test coverage minimum
**Scale/Scope**: 20 total commands (6 existing + 14 new), 5 user stories (P1–P5), web-only

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| I. Specification-First | ✅ PASS | spec.md exists with 5 prioritized user stories (P1–P5), Given-When-Then acceptance scenarios, clear scope boundaries, and edge cases |
| II. Template-Driven Workflow | ✅ PASS | All artifacts follow canonical templates from `.specify/templates/` |
| III. Agent-Orchestrated Execution | ✅ PASS | speckit.plan agent produces plan.md, research.md, data-model.md, contracts/, quickstart.md; hands off to speckit.tasks |
| IV. Test Optionality | ✅ PASS | Tests are not explicitly mandated in spec; existing test patterns (registry.test.ts, settings.test.ts, help.test.ts) provide convention for new commands. Tests should follow existing patterns when implementing. |
| V. Simplicity and DRY | ✅ PASS | New commands follow existing registry pattern — no new abstractions needed. Handler files organized by domain to avoid single-file bloat while maintaining DRY. Passthrough commands reuse existing backend forwarding mechanism. |

**Gate Result**: ✅ ALL PASS — proceed to Phase 0.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-copilot-slash-commands/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output — resolved unknowns and design decisions
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output — entity definitions and relationships
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output — developer quickstart guide
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output — API contracts
│   └── slash-commands-api.yaml
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (NOT created by speckit.plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
solune/
├── frontend/
│   └── src/
│       ├── lib/commands/
│       │   ├── registry.ts            # Extend with 14 new registerCommand() calls
│       │   ├── types.ts               # Extend CommandContext if needed for new capabilities
│       │   └── handlers/
│       │       ├── help.ts            # Existing — no changes needed
│       │       ├── settings.ts        # Existing — add /experimental handler here
│       │       ├── agent.ts           # Existing — no changes needed
│       │       ├── session.ts         # NEW — /clear, /compact, /context handlers
│       │       ├── monitoring.ts      # NEW — /diff, /usage, /share, /feedback handlers
│       │       └── advanced.ts        # NEW — /mcp, /plan, /model handlers
│       ├── hooks/
│       │   ├── useCommands.ts         # May need CommandContext extensions
│       │   └── useChat.ts             # May need /clear integration with clearChat
│       ├── pages/
│       │   └── HelpPage.tsx           # Auto-updates from registry — no changes needed
│       └── services/
│           └── api.ts                 # Already has clearMessages; no new endpoints for local commands
└── backend/
    └── src/
        ├── api/
        │   └── chat.py               # Already has clear_messages endpoint; passthrough handling in send_message
        ├── models/
        │   └── chat.py               # No schema changes needed for basic commands
        └── services/
            ├── chat_store.py          # Existing — already supports clear_messages
            └── signal_chat.py         # Existing — passthrough commands handled by AI service
```

**Structure Decision**: Web application structure (Option 2). The existing `solune/frontend` + `solune/backend` layout is used. New frontend handler files are organized by domain (session, monitoring, advanced) following the existing pattern of `settings.ts` grouping related commands. No new backend routes are required — passthrough commands use the existing `send_message` endpoint, and `/clear` uses the existing `clear_messages` endpoint.
